seoTitle: How to Use Canva to Create SVGs for Web and Branding
metaDescription: Design and export scalable vector graphics in Canva for logos, icons, and illustrations that stay crisp at any size across web and marketing projects.
intro: SVGs stay sharp at any size, which makes them a go-to format for logos, icons, and web graphics. This guide walks through designing custom vector-style graphics in Canva and exporting them as SVG files ready to use in branding, marketing, or web projects.

steps.heading: Start a new Canva design for your graphic
steps.body: Choose a blank canvas or template as the base for the icon, logo, or illustration you plan to turn into an SVG.

steps.heading: Build your design using shapes and elements
steps.body: Combine Canva's built-in shapes, icons, and graphic elements to construct your visual, keeping it simple so it exports cleanly.

steps.heading: Adjust colors and layout
steps.body: Fine-tune colors, spacing, and proportions so the graphic matches your brand style before exporting.

steps.heading: Export the design as an SVG file
steps.body: Use Canva's download options to export your finished design in SVG format, keeping it scalable for any use case.

steps.heading: Apply the SVG to real projects
steps.body: Use the exported file in websites, marketing materials, or social graphics where a crisp, resizable image is needed.

faq.question: Can any Canva design be exported as an SVG?
faq.answer: Not every design type supports SVG export cleanly, so it works best with simpler graphics built from shapes and vector elements rather than complex photo-based layouts.

faq.question: Why use SVG instead of PNG or JPG?
faq.answer: SVGs scale without losing quality, which makes them ideal for logos and icons that need to look sharp at any size, from a favicon to a large banner.

faq.question: Is SVG export available on Canva's free plan?
faq.answer: SVG export availability can depend on your Canva plan, so it's worth checking your account's download options if you don't see it immediately.

faq.question: Can I edit an SVG after exporting it from Canva?
faq.answer: Once exported, further edits typically require a vector editing tool, so it's best to finalize the design in Canva before downloading.
